title: Polestar has been muscled out of the US market
content: Polestar won't be allowed to sell its electric vehicles model year 2027 and beyond in the US after the federal government denied the company's request for authorization under a new rule banning vehicles with software from China. title: Is this solar e-bike a good idea or sophisticated e-waste?
content: I like the idea of a solar-powered electric bike, but I don't think anyone should buy the new Phosgo Go5 - not yet, anyway. title: Zoox’s purpose-built robotaxi is getting a refresh
content: Zoox, the autonomous vehicle company owned by Amazon, unveiled a new look for its boxy, bidirectional robotaxi, calling it the "next evolution" of the vehicle intended for mass production. title: I drove the Slate Truck — there’s more to it than EV minimalism
content: With its new pickup, Slate Auto is making a simple bet: price matters more than almost anything else. title: The Slate Auto pickup truck starts at $24,950
content: We now know the price of Slate Auto's affordable American-made electric truck, almost a year after the company warned it wouldn't hit its initial "under $20,000" target price. title: Tesla claims driver ‘manually overrode self-driving’ in deadly Texas crash
content: Tesla is pushing back on claims that its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system caused a fatal Texas crash, where a speeding Model 3 barreled into a home, killing a 76-year-old woman inside. title: Formula E’s new season is starting to look more like F1
content: The next season of Formula E will feature a new race format and three new race locations when it starts in December to go with the new Gen4 electric cars. content: Amflow, the e-bike brand spun out of DJI, just announced its TL Carbon, a do-it-all "eSUV" suitable for both bikepacking adventures and dropping the kid at daycare on your cycle to work.
